title: Delhi High Court lays down guidelines for quashing POCSO cases involving consensual sex
description: In a significant ruling, the Delhi High Court on Thursday laid down a set of guidelines for quashing cases under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act (POCSO Act) when the victim denies having suffered any actual harm. Justice Anup Jairam Bhambhani observed that in many POCSO cases, there might be a de juré victim, a victim as formally recognised by law, but not a de facto or actual victim who has suffered any harm or loss.
